tagline: Two fixes to the patch-notes panel itself — it now appears as an overlay over the dashboard (no more separate tab) and scrolls properly when there's a lot to read.

highlights.text: Patch notes now overlay the dashboard

sections.title: Patch notes appear as an overlay, not a new tab
sections.subtitle: The 'What's New' panel now opens *over* the Agents dashboard instead of as a separate editor tab.
sections.body:
When v0.0.4 introduced the patch-notes panel, it opened as its own editor tab — which felt like a parallel workspace instead of a notification. Now it appears as a **modal overlay** with the dashboard softly blurred behind it. Closing the panel (× / ESC / clicking the backdrop / **Got it**) drops you straight back to the agents grid where you were.

If the dashboard isn't open when an update lands, it'll be opened first; the overlay then appears on top.
sections.techDetails: Removed the standalone `src/panels/ChangelogPanel.ts` (and its `agentCode.changelog` webview view type). The dashboard panel now exposes a static `showChangelog(...)` that posts a `changelog/show` HostMessage carrying the bundle. The dashboard webview keeps the bundle in local state and renders `<ChangelogOverlay />` on top of `<AgentsDashboard />`, following the same pattern as `SettingsModal` / `UsageModal`. Dismiss/disable messages still round-trip through the host to update `globalState[agentCode.changelog.lastSeenVersion]` and the `agentCode.showPatchNotesOnUpdate` setting.

sections.title: Scrolling works on long changelogs
sections.subtitle: If the panel is taller than the viewport, you can now actually read the bottom of it.
sections.body: The previous version capped the panel at `min-height: 100vh` inside a `body { overflow: hidden }` — meaning anything past the fold got clipped without a scrollbar. The new overlay uses a fixed-height card with `overflow-y-auto` on the scrolling region, so long releases (multiple entries, multi-version skips) scroll properly inside the modal.
sections.techDetails: Card frame is `flex flex-col` with `max-h-[92vh]`; the content region is `min-h-0 flex-1 overflow-y-auto` so the footer stays pinned to the bottom of the card and the entries scroll under it.
